title: AMC Theatres Posts Higher Revenue on Attendance Rebound in Latest Quarter
text: AMC Entertainment Holdings demonstrated significant improvement in its first quarter 2026 financial results, driven by increased theater attendance and higher revenue across both domestic and international markets. U.S. attendance climbed 14 percent to 30.7 million moviegoers, compared to 27 million in the prior year period. International markets also performed well, with attendance rising 12.6 percent to 16.8 million patrons versus 15 million previously. Overall revenues reached $1.04 billion, up from $862.5 million year-over-year. The company's net loss narrowed significantly to $117 million from $202.1 million in the comparable quarter, while adjusted EBITDA improved by $96 million. Admissions revenue jumped to $578.4 million from $473.5 million, while food and beverage sales increased to $347.3 million from $283.4 million. CEO Adam Aron noted that long-term debt now stands at approximately $3.9 billion, marking substantial progress from the $5 billion debt load before the pandemic and the $6 billion peak in late 2020. Aron expressed confidence about ongoing momentum, citing upcoming releases and renewed studio commitments to theatrical windows of at least 45 days before streaming availability.
